attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 7th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 303->353. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: the routine longitudinal management of a stable congenital/genetic/developmental disability is low-tier (self-care + specialist/allied-health/MDT referral + safety-net), while acute decompensations and time-critical complications are tiered to true acuity (T3-T5) with mandatory escalation edges — autonomic dysreflexia in spinal cord injury (T5/000), metabolic crises (MSUD/galactosaemia/mitochondrial/peroxisomal/hypoglycaemia), aspiration pneumonia, progressive neuromuscular respiratory failure, major burns, pressure-injury sepsis, status epilepticus, decompensated congenital heart disease, hypocalcaemic tetany, and demand myocardial ischaemia in supravalvular aortic stenosis. Paediatric no-dose hard limit honoured throughout (no synthesised mg/kg or weight-based dose; paediatric and unknown-weight cases flagged for in-person/specialist prescribing), no autonomous diagnosis, diagnostic-overshadowing encoded as the key error trap. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) DCD-016 source file titled 'Perthes' but the clinical picture is developmental dysplasia of the hip in a 4-month-old — authored correctly as DDH, source title is a note-quirk only; (b) the T5-heavy tier profile (T3x9/T4x10/T5x31) is accepted as honest for a folder authored entirely as acute-complication presentations; (c) DCD-050 Williams syndrome panic attack tiered T5 is accepted (fixed supravalvular aortic stenosis + tachycardia -> demand myocardial ischaemia, the note's own primary differential is acute MI).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DCD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Unspecified cleft palate with unilateral cleft lip

transform_flags: Patient age is 4 months (recorded as age 0 years, age_band infant); paediatric_context populated.
Sex inferred male from the source note's pronouns ('his'/'him') — confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context not stated in the source — geographic_context omitted.
paediatric_context.informant set to 'guardian' (a parent) — the source says 'parents' present without specifying which parent reported; confirm on review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person home visit (clinician otoscopy, full vitals, guided oral rehydration, oral ibuprofen given, amoxicillin prescribed). Reprojected to the telehealth-correct action — recognise a vulnerable dehydrating infant and escalate to urgent in-person paediatric assessment; clinician-only findings (otoscopy, auscultation, vitals, capillary refill, fontanelle, SpO2) sealed into 10/11 and telehealth_limits.

PAEDIATRIC DOSING NOT SYNTHESISED (hard limit): the source's weight-based ibuprofen and amoxicillin doses and the paracetamol dose are NOT reproduced — dose_route_frequency omitted and flagged for in-person, weight-based prescribing.

Ibuprofen re-classified 'not_indicated_here' for this clinically dehydrated infant (NSAID renal-perfusion risk) despite the source clinician using it — flagged as a clinical-judgment reprojection for reviewer confirmation.

Baseline tier set T4 (urgent same-day in-person/ED). The in-person source clinician managed at home with 12h GP review; telehealth cannot examine or cannulate a vulnerable dehydrated lethargic infant, so the conservative telehealth-correct disposition is urgent in-person assessment. Confirm on clinician review.
case_id specialty assigned PAEDS (primary; secondary ID for the infective driver) and difficulty atypical_presentation_high_risk (DD=04); source category DCD.
case_id SEQ is provisional (00012 from source DCD-012) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-PAEDS-04 bucket at ingest.
All SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes are candidates (unverified_pending_terminology_receipt); AMT drug entries use candidate SNOMED substance codes pending terminology receipts.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
